title: Coniogramme spinulosa
taxonomy: Coniogramme spinulosa is classified in the family Coniogrammeaceae, which is a small family of ferns. This plant belongs to the division Pteridophyta. Within the plant kingdom, it is classified as a vascular plant. Coniogramme spinulosa is further classified into the genus Coniogramme and the species Coniogramme spinulosa. The classification of Coniogramme spinulosa is also known as a fern of the family Coniogrammeaceae.

intro: Coniogramme spinulosa, also known as the spiny coniogramme, is a species of fern native to Southeast Asia, characterized by its unique, spiny rhizome and delicate fronds. Traditionally, Coniogramme spinulosa has been used in herbal medicine to treat various health conditions, including fever, rheumatism, and digestive issues, due to its anti-inflammatory, antibacterial, and antiviral properties. The bioactive constituents of Coniogramme spinulosa include flavonoids, saponins, and phenolic acids, which contribute to its medicinal properties. In herbal medicine, Coniogramme spinulosa is often prepared as a decoction, tea, or tincture, and is also used in traditional Chinese medicine, where it is valued for its ability to promote detoxification and boost the immune system.
